name: claude
concept: "THE LOOM THAT WEAVES ITSELF" — the entire surface is a single continuous WebGL loom. The Clotho weave isn't an illustration on the page; it IS the page. Visitors arrive at a dark void where a single luminous thread (AI-START-HERE) hangs vertically like a warp thread. As the story progresses, threads multiply, cross, knot, snap, and re-splice — the fabric of TELOS literally weaving itself in front of you. The central conceit: a loom is honest machinery. It shows its tension. When adversarial workshops STALEMATE, two threads pull against each other and the shuttle physically stops — a visible, held tension the visitor must resolve by reading both sides. When Daedalus's gate-passing plan hits ground truth and fails, a woven section UNRAVELS on screen — frames of completed fabric pulling apart — before being rewoven correctly. Corrections aren't hidden; they're scars in the cloth, rendered as visibly re-spliced threads with a different sheen (iridescent shader on 'superseded' seams). The tagline moment: the camera pulls back at the end to reveal the full woven tapestry is a content-addressed graph that 'CAN'T BE LOST' — every thread hash-labeled, the fabric queryable.
journey: A HYBRID of scroll-driven cinematics and free exploration, structured as five 'sheds' (the loom term for the gap the shuttle passes through). (1) THE FIRST THREAD: WebGL hero — one thread, bold typographic overlay ('TELOS built TELOS. It did not trust itself.'). Scroll advances the shuttle. (2) THE ILIAD SHEDS: each enrollment quest is a shed-opening — threads separate, the visitor's scroll drives the shuttle through, and the actual authored institutional-memory record slides in as a reading panel pinned to the thread it produced. (3) THE STALEMATE: scroll STOPS working (deliberately — a scroll-jack with clear affordance). Two adversarial workshop positions render as opposing thread tensions; the visitor must open BOTH argument panels (real record excerpts) before the tension resolves and scroll unlocks. Builder != certifier made kinetic. (4) THE UNRAVELING: Daedalus's comprehensive plan weaves a beautiful, gate-stamped section — then code hits ground truth and it visibly unravels; the DECISIONS records that corrected it appear as re-splice points. (5) THE TAPESTRY: camera pulls back, scroll ends, and the surface converts to FREE EXPLORATION mode — the full Clotho weave as a navigable 3D fabric (the 3D-walkthrough plugin drives camera paths here), with filter chips per edge type, a timeline scrubber (D3) that re-weaves history at any point, and export/report controls. Every chapter is deep-linkable (URL-addressed camera+scrub state) — critical for the E2E blade and for shareability.
graph_treatment: The Clotho graph is rendered as WOVEN FABRIC, not a ball-of-string node-link diagram (the #1 cliché to kill). Mapping: nodes = knots/intersections; typed edges = thread BEHAVIORS with distinct shader treatments — 'depends-on' = warp threads (structural, vertical, load-bearing, thicker); 'verified-by' = weft threads that pass a glowing shuttle-pulse along them on hover; 'introduced-by' = threads that visibly emerge from an author-record spool in the margin; 'supersedes' = the signature move: the old thread remains in the fabric but desaturated and frayed, with the new thread spliced over it — history is never deleted, only overwoven (this IS the 'can't be lost' thesis, rendered). Content-addressing is surfaced: hover any knot and its hash renders in monospace micro-type along the thread itself (SDF text on curve). The graph's real structure informs IA: the five journey chapters are computed from the graph's actual topological eras (cluster-by-introduction-time), so the story spine is derived from the data, not hand-faked — and the free-exploration tapestry at the end is the SAME scene graph the story used, just with camera constraints released. One dataset, two modes, zero bait-and-switch.
signature_interaction: THE SHUTTLE. A persistent, physical-feeling shuttle object (the loom's carrier) is the visitor's cursor-companion and progress avatar. Scroll velocity maps to shuttle speed with spring physics (drei/framer-motion-3d springs); the shuttle trails a live thread — YOUR visit literally weaves a faint 'visitor thread' through the fabric, which appears in the final tapestry (your path through the story becomes part of the cloth, ephemerally). The signature ANIMATION is the UNRAVELING: a custom vertex shader animates woven geometry decomposing along its actual edge order (reverse topological sort of the graph section), threads whipping loose with verlet-style physics, then re-weaving along the corrected DECISIONS path. It's the emotional peak — a plan that passed every gate, coming apart beautifully and honestly. Secondary signature: 'PULL A THREAD' — in exploration mode, click-and-drag any thread and the fabric deforms with real tension propagation along depends-on edges, physically demonstrating what depends on what; release and it snaps back with an audible-feeling spring. Dependency analysis as a tactile toy.
tech: React + Vite + Tailwind (LEXI-consistent). Three.js via react-three-fiber + drei. Threads: instanced tube geometry along Catmull-Rom curves with custom GLSL — vertex shader handles weave undulation, tension deformation, and the unravel; fragment shader handles sheen/iridescence for splice scars (thin-film approximation) + SDF hash-text along curves. Physics: lightweight verlet solver in a Web Worker (position-based dynamics, ~2k active threads max; distant fabric baked to static instanced mesh — LOD by camera distance). Scroll: Lenis + a scrub timeline (GSAP ScrollTrigger or theatre.js) driving both camera and shader uniforms; the 3D-walkthrough plugin supplies camera splines for exploration mode. D3 owns the timeline scrubber and 2D detail panels (record readers, decision diffs) rendered as DOM overlays — NOT in-canvas — so every interactive element (buttons, dropdowns, exports, panels) is real, semantic, testable HTML. This is the E2E strategy: WebGL is presentation; ALL interaction contracts live in DOM with data-testids and deterministic state (URL-driven, no RNG in interactive paths; visitor-thread randomness seeded and excluded from assertions). CWV: canvas defers behind an LCP-optimized static hero frame (compressed poster + font preload), progressive graph hydration, zero layout shift (canvas is fixed-position), INP protected by keeping physics off main thread. Full reduced-motion mode: weave renders pre-completed, scroll becomes plain reading flow.
why_soty: SOTY juries reward three things this direction nails: (1) A METAPHOR WITH INTEGRITY — the loom isn't decoration; Clotho IS the spinner of thread in myth, the graph IS the fabric, and 'supersedes-but-never-deletes' IS content-addressing made visible. Judges can feel when a metaphor is load-bearing versus applied. (2) AN HONEST EMOTIONAL ARC — most product stories are triumphal; this one has a scene where the hero's plan publicly unravels. The stalemate scroll-lock and the unraveling sequence are the kind of 'I've never seen a site DO that' moments that win awards, and they're honest to the source material, not invented drama. (3) CRAFT DEPTH ON BOTH BLADES — bespoke shader work + physics for the Eye, and a deliberately DOM-first interaction architecture so the deterministic E2E blade has real, boring, assertable HTML to bite into. It clears the LEXI aesthetic bar (dark, data-forward, WebGL hero) while being unmistakably its own thing — nobody else's dev-story site is a self-weaving loom whose final state is a queryable knowledge graph.
biggest_risk: THE STALEMATE SCROLL-LOCK (chapter 3) is the single biggest risk — it's the same mechanism as my proudest moment. Deliberately breaking scroll to force engagement with both adversarial positions is either the awarded moment or the site's fatal flaw: if the affordance isn't instantly legible, visitors think the site is broken (bounce, rage-scroll), reduced-motion/keyboard/screen-reader users can get trapped (a11y failure), and any nondeterminism in the unlock condition (did both panels register as 'read'?) makes the E2E flake — failing the functional blade on my signature feature. Mitigation is designable (explicit 'tension gauge' UI, skip affordance after 5s, unlock state in URL/store so tests assert it deterministically) but I won't pretend it away: I've bet the emotional core of the site on interrupting the one gesture every visitor trusts. If that bet fails, the whole 'honest tension' thesis reads as a gimmick that jailed the user.

name: codex
biggest_risk: The canonical navigation is also a live WebGL graph, so the functional blade is the danger: picking, camera transitions, and shader-driven state can become nondeterministic across devices and make controls difficult to test or access. Mitigation must be architectural, not cosmetic: every graph action maps to a stable semantic command and mirrored DOM control, with fixed camera states and animation-disable test mode. If that contract is compromised, the experience becomes an impressive but unusable demo.
concept: THE LOOM ON TRIAL — a forensic, room-scale weaving machine built from TELOS’s institutional memory. The Clotho graph forms the fabric; AI-START-HERE is its first thread, Iliad quests establish the warp, Daedalus plans become drafted patterns, councils apply tension, and decisions knot or cut connections. The visitor is not shown a victory reel: they operate the machine as an examiner. Contradictions visibly strain the weave; STALEMATE locks the loom; superseded claims remain as scars. The climax is the plan that cleared every gate but failed against running code: the immaculate fabric tears open, exposes ground truth beneath it, and is rewoven into the workflow contract—an architecture whose reasoning cannot be lost.
graph_treatment: The visualization is generated from the real Clotho node and edge types, never a decorative particle cloud. A worker computes a constrained layered layout: time runs through the loom’s depth, institutional domains form vertical warp bands, and typed edges become differently behaving threads—depends-on bears tension, verified-by clamps nodes together, introduced-by emits provenance fibers, and supersedes leaves a dim ghost strand rather than deleting history. Density resolves progressively: distant records appear as woven topology; approaching a knot reveals titles, status, authorship, evidence, decision excerpts, and outgoing relations. A persistent 2D Evidence Ledger mirrors the selected subgraph, supports search and filtering, provides permanent URLs, and exports the exact visible evidence set as JSON, CSV, or a printable report.
journey: There is no conventional long page. Visitors enter a dark inspection chamber facing the complete suspended weave and choose one of six named examination passes: Origin, Enrollment, Adversary, Plan, Ground Truth, or Contract. Each pass is a directed route derived from graph relationships rather than a manually faked slideshow. The camera travels only between deterministic stations; at each station the visitor can accept the authored route, branch through a connected record, open the Evidence Ledger, or return to the full-system view. A typographic transcript at the edge of the chamber narrates what TELOS believed, what challenged it, what was decided, and what reality later disproved. Progress is measured as evidence examined—not percentage scrolled—and every chapter ends with an explicit claim/evidence/correction card.
signature_interaction: PULL THE THREAD. Selecting any claim lets the visitor physically draw its thread toward them. The loom responds with a GPU tension simulation: supporting evidence tightens and brightens, dependencies transmit force across the structure, unresolved challenges oscillate, and contradictory or missing verification causes fibers to split. At the pivotal ‘passed every gate’ record, pulling the approved plan creates a moment of apparent perfect alignment—then a ground-truth code node rises from below, applies incompatible tension, and tears the surface along the exact failed assumptions. Releasing the pull does not reset history: the weave repairs with luminous correction stitches, making reconsideration and provenance permanently visible. Haptics, spatial audio, typography, and the Evidence Ledger synchronize to the same state machine.
tech: React and TypeScript provide the semantic application shell; React Three Fiber/Three.js render the loom with custom GLSL ribbon shaders, signed-distance-field labels, GPU instancing, and a bounded spring/tension simulation. D3 runs graph stratification and route generation inside a Web Worker, with layouts cached by weave revision. XState owns every interaction, camera station, panel, filter, export, and reduced-motion transition so WebGL never becomes the source of truth. Content is compiled from the authored records and Clotho data into validated, addressable story packets. Performance strategy: one canvas, aggressive instancing, texture atlases, adaptive DPR, occlusion/LOD, no layout-triggering animation, and a static/WebGL-lite mode for constrained devices. Playwright drives a generated manifest of every semantic command in animation-disabled mode, while visual tests separately exercise full shader motion.
why_soty: It makes TELOS’s governing idea spatially legible: knowledge survives because claims, challenges, evidence, corrections, and provenance remain connected. The central spectacle is not an ornamental orb but the real institutional graph behaving according to its meaning. The tear-and-repair sequence gives the story a memorable cinematic image while refusing triumphalist mythology: stalemates are structural locks, superseded decisions remain visible, and a formally successful plan is shown failing against reality. Monumental typography, forensic data detail, restrained spatial sound, and a tactile shader language create visual authorship; deterministic stations, evidence-level deep links, and the mirrored ledger keep the work useful rather than merely experiential. It has a SOTY-scale signature moment, but the interaction itself is the argument.

name: grok
concept: Narcissus Crucible: a first-person 3D walkthrough inside a living ferrofluid knowledge-weave that only solidifies into architecture after you personally re-enact every stalemate, correction, and ground-truth failure. The surface is not a site about TELOS — it is TELOS’s own adversarial mirror: builder claims appear as fluid chrome, certifier doubts as black ice, and only survived collisions crystallize into permanent graph edges.
journey: Visitor spawns in absolute void (AI-START-HERE monowire). A single filament of institutional memory pulls them forward into the Iliad enrollment quests as orbiting particle swarms. Crossing a depends-on edge hurls them into a Daedalus workshop chamber where plans are molten and must be stress-tested. Councils appear as multi-voice resonance fields; retrospectives as time-reversed supersedes tunnels. The path is non-linear and graph-native: you physically walk typed edges, and dead-end stalemates force backtracking until a verified-by node unlocks the next chamber. Final descent crystallizes the entire weave into the ‘unlosable’ architecture vault — only after the visitor has witnessed at least one plan that passed every gate yet shattered on contact with code.
graph_treatment: Clotho is the entire spatial IA and renderer. Typed nodes (Decision, Stalemate, Workshop, GroundTruth, Supersession) are volumetric ferrofluid orbs with distinct shader signatures; edges are tensioned light-filaments whose thickness and turbulence encode strength and conflict. The graph is force-directed in 3D but pinned to a narrative spine so story order remains legible. Selecting any node extrudes its full institutional-memory record as a floating, typographically perfect dossier while dimming unrelated subgraphs. The weave literally re-weaves itself when the visitor triggers a historical correction — edges snap, nodes fission, and the camera is thrown through the resulting shockwave.
signature_interaction: ‘Force the Stalemate’: grab any Decision orb, yank it into the Crucible Arena (a circular void that opens underfoot). The orb splits into dual ferrofluid avatars — Builder Claim vs Certifier Doubt — that collide under real-time fluid simulation. Visitor must hold the conflict in tension (mouse/touch drag against simulated viscosity) until the historical outcome resolves: either crystallization into a verified edge with a satisfying metallic ring, or collapse into black-ice shards that rewrite upstream history. Animation is shader-driven ferrofluid + particle debris + diegetic audio of the original council recordings pitch-shifted into the impact.
tech: React 19 + Vite + Tailwind + React Three Fiber + custom GLSL ferrofluid/raymarched shaders (Three.js), D3 for secondary timeline and dossier layouts, GSAP for camera pathing and dossier transitions, pre-computed graph layouts streamed as compressed binary for instant load, 3D-walkthrough plugin for optional guided rails, aggressive LOD + occlusion culling + instanced edges so the full weave stays 60 fps on mid-tier laptops. All interactive hit-targets remain DOM-accessible overlays for the deterministic E2E blade.
why_soty: It turns dry governance archaeology into a visceral, body-scale experience no marketing site has attempted: you feel the cost of every rubber-stamp avoided. Extreme visual signature (living ferrofluid graph that literally remembers its wounds), honest narrative that celebrates failure, and technical bravura (shader-driven narrative state, sub-second graph re-weaves, perfect CWV) create the rare Awwwards trifecta of beauty, meaning, and craft that cannot be screen-shotted — it must be walked.
biggest_risk: Functional E2E blade: deterministic testing of free-form 3D grab-and-collide stalemate interactions plus dynamic graph re-writes is brutally hard; flaky hit-testing or non-deterministic fluid settles could fail the entire gate even if the aesthetic is flawless.

name: gemini
concept: The Adversarial Loom - A macroscopic, tension-loaded 3D cable of dark liquid metal and glowing glass fiber. It visually represents the friction of TELOS's creation: smooth threads for consensus, violent snaps and glowing knots for stalemates, and fused industrial slag for reality-correcting code. It rejects the sanitized tech-launch myth for a brutalist, honest aesthetic.
journey: Z-axis descent into the weave. The user operates a digital shuttle, dragging the camera down the timeline. Passing Iliad quests introduces threads; Daedalus workshops braid them. At stalemates, the descent forcibly halts, requiring the user to inspect the architectural disagreement to unknot it. It is an interactive excavation, not a passive scroll.
graph_treatment: Clotho is rendered as a topographical, physics-driven textile in WebGL. Nodes are intersection points where threads fuse. Edges are the threads. Custom thermal shaders indicate adversarial friction (red/orange for supersedes/stalemates). The user can pull back to view the macro-weave or zoom into a single atomic thread of the graph.
signature_interaction: The Stalemate Fracture. When encountering a historical roadblock (e.g. a plan failing ground truth), the scroll halts with a visual glitch and the thread shatters. The user must click and drag the fractured ends to re-splice them, triggering an aggressive shader explosion of ferrofluid slag fusing together, which spawns the post-mortem report in sharp typography.
tech: React wrapping a React Three Fiber canvas. Custom GLSL shaders for liquid metal thread materials, utilizing Raymarching for volumetric friction points. D3.js runs headless in a Web Worker to calculate the physics-based graph layout, feeding Float32Arrays directly to WebGL InstancedMeshes. This ensures 60fps rendering of 10000+ Clotho nodes while the main thread handles React UI.
why_soty: It turns institutional failure, engineering arguments, and architectural pivots into breathtaking, aggressive interactive art. It seamlessly blends high-performance WebGL data visualization with uncompromising narrative honesty, proving that complex, adversarial governance can be aesthetically transcendent.
biggest_risk: The deterministic E2E testing gate. Tying critical narrative progression and document retrieval to bespoke, physics-driven WebGL interactions (like dragging fractured threads in 3D space) makes deterministic Playwright/Cypress testing incredibly fragile. If the functional blade cannot reliably assert these non-DOM interactions, the proposal completely fails the gate.
